openmeter/billing/charges/flatfee/servic.purpose: Owns flat-fee credit allocation and correction mechanics — persisting credit realization records and their lineage segments. Must not make state-machine decisions; all charge lifecycle transitions happen in the parent charges.Service layer.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() before construction
patterns.description: All deps declared in Config; Config.Validate() collects nil-check errors via errors.Join before New() returns. Never construct Service directly.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Input structs carry Validate() called at method entry
patterns.description: Each method input (AllocateCreditsOnlyInput, CorrectAllCreditRealizationsInput, AccrueInvoiceUsageInput, StartCreditThenInvoiceRunInput) has Validate() checking zero-values, negative amounts, and cross-field invariants; call it at the top of every method before any adapter call.
patterns.example: func (s *Service) AllocateCreditsOnly(ctx, in AllocateCreditsOnlyInput) (AllocateCreditsOnlyResult, error) { if err := in.Validate(); err != nil { return AllocateCreditsOnlyResult{}, err } ... }

patterns.name: Currency rounding before validation and sum assertion
patterns.description: Call RoundToPrecision on monetary amounts before Validate() and before sum equality checks to avoid float false negatives: round, validate, then assert sum equality.

patterns.name: createCreditAllocations always triples write+lineage
patterns.description: The private createCreditAllocations always calls adapter.CreateCreditAllocations, then lineage.CreateInitialLineages, then lineage.PersistCorrectionLineageSegments within the caller's transaction; skipping either lineage step breaks correction lookups.

patterns.name: Handler delegates allocation-splitting decisions
patterns.description: Service does not compute how credits split across ledger accounts; it calls flatfee.Handler methods (OnCreditsOnlyUsageAccrued, OnCreditsOnlyUsageAccruedCorrection, OnAssignedToInvoice, OnInvoiceUsageAccrued) and only validates sum equality afterward.
patterns.example: creditAllocations, _ := s.handler.OnCreditsOnlyUsageAccrued(ctx, input); allocated := cc.RoundToPrecision(creditAllocations.Sum()); if !allocated.Equal(in.Amount) { return ..., models.NewGenericValidationError(...) }

patterns.name: Multi-step writes wrapped in transaction.Run
patterns.description: Methods combining several adapter writes (CreateCurrentRun + CreateCreditAllocations + UpdateRealizationRun + UpsertDetailedLines) wrap all steps in transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, ...) to guarantee atomicity.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (StartCreditThenInvoiceRunResult, error) { s.adapter.CreateCurrentRun(...); s.createCreditAllocations(...); s.adapter.UpdateRealizationRun(...) })

patterns.name: Sum-mismatch returns GenericValidationError, not internal error
patterns.description: When allocated sum != expected after rounding, return models.NewGenericValidationError (not fmt.Errorf) so GenericErrorEncoder maps it to HTTP 400.
patterns.example: if !allocated.Equal(in.Amount) { return AllocateCreditsOnlyResult{}, models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("credit allocations do not match total [charge_id=%s]", ...)) }

key_file_guides.role: Service struct, Config, Config.Validate(), New(), and the private createCreditAllocations helper enforcing the triple-write invariant.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New exported methods must follow Config.Validate + Input.Validate + transaction.Run; never call adapter.CreateCreditAllocations without lineage.PersistCorrectionLineageSegments.

key_file_guides.role: AllocateCreditsOnly: rounds amount, delegates split to handler.OnCreditsOnlyUsageAccrued, asserts sum equality, persists realizations and updates run totals in transaction.Run.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Skipping the sum equality assertion or doing arithmetic without RoundToPrecision.

key_file_guides.role: CorrectAllCredits: loads active lineage segments, delegates corrections to handler.OnCreditsOnlyUsageAccruedCorrection via creditrealization.CorrectAll, persists correction realizations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Calling createCreditAllocations without first loading lineage via lineage.LoadActiveSegmentsByRealizationID.

key_file_guides.role: StartCreditThenInvoiceRun: creates run, allocates via handler.OnAssignedToInvoice, generates/merges detailed lines via ratingService, persists in one transaction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate() requires line.ChargeID == charge.ID and line.InvoiceID == invoice.ID — callers must satisfy both.

key_file_guides.role: AccrueInvoiceUsage: calls handler.OnInvoiceUsageAccrued to post a ledger transaction, persists invoiced usage, then marks the run immutable.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate() rejects inputs where currentRun.AccruedUsage != nil (already accrued) or run IDs mismatch the line/invoice IDs.

anti_patterns: Making state-machine decisions (charge status transitions) here — lifecycle transitions belong in the parent charges.Service layer.
Calling adapter.CreateCreditAllocations without both lineage.CreateInitialLineages and lineage.PersistCorrectionLineageSegments in the same transaction.
Currency arithmetic without CurrencyCalculator.RoundToPrecision before validation or sum comparison.
Returning fmt.Errorf for allocation sum mismatches instead of models.NewGenericValidationError (breaks HTTP 400 mapping).
Skipping Config.Validate() or Input.Validate() before adapter calls.

decisions.decision: Handler delegates allocation-splitting; Service only validates sum and persists.
decisions.rationale: Different flat-fee strategies (immediate, pro-rated, credit-then-invoice) vary split logic; keeping it in flatfee.Handler keeps realization persistence strategy-agnostic.

decisions.decision: Lineage persistence is mandatory and co-located in createCreditAllocations.
decisions.rationale: Realization records without lineage segments break correction lookups; coupling adapter write + lineage in one method makes it structurally impossible to forget lineage steps.

decisions.decision: Config+Validate pattern instead of variadic functional options.
decisions.rationale: Consistent with the billing/charges constructor convention; surfaces missing-dependency errors at New() rather than at first method invocation.

code_examples.scenario: Allocate credits for a flat-fee charge, persist realizations and lineage, assert sum equality
